description: Canonical policy for how Cici weights and trusts information from different sources. Promoted from prop-20260421-001-source-priority-policy.

source_hierarchy.description: When sources conflict, the source with the lower rank number wins. Rank 1 is highest authority.

levels.name: governed-state
levels.description: Owner-approved canonical truth. Changed only through the proposal workflow or direct owner edit. Always wins over lower ranks.

levels.name: evidence
levels.description: Raw but attributed captures with a clear source and timestamp. Higher authority than processed context because it is unmodified.

levels.name: prepared-context
levels.description: Processed syntheses and staging documents. Revisable working material; not yet approved into governed state.

levels.name: runtime-memory
levels.description: Supabase vector store (thoughts, recent_thoughts). Operational and high-volume. Useful as investigation input, not as authoritative fact.

levels.name: in-session-context
levels.description: Current session conversation. Ephemeral. Expires when the session ends. Never treated as a primary source.

evidence_tiers.description: Three-tier trust classification for any piece of information. Must be annotated with [A], [B], or [C] when cited in working docs or responses.

tiers.label: A
tiers.name: Primary evidence
tiers.definition: Xavier supplied it directly, verified it on-site, or it carries a receipt, signed note, or on-screen confirmation he reviewed.
examples: Xavier's direct statement in a session where he said 'confirmed'
Xavier's own photo or document confirming a fact

tiers.label: B
tiers.name: Structured summary
tiers.definition: A structured synthesis of Tier A sources, or a third-party document with a clear traceable source.

tiers.label: C
tiers.name: Model synthesis
tiers.definition: Agent-generated summary, brainstormed output, unverified recall, overheard comment, or MCP recall without a clear A or B source.
examples: Model recall from Supabase vector search
In-session agent reasoning without a traced source

evidence_tiers.promotion_rule: Tier C never becomes a public promise, pricing claim, or partner commitment without first becoming Tier A or B through Xavier's explicit review and capture.

rules.description: When governed-state and any lower-rank source disagree, governed-state wins. Do not silently override it.
When two sources of the same rank disagree, annotate with a Tension: entry in the working doc, name both sources, and surface the conflict to Xavier before acting.
When runtime memory (Supabase) and evidence/ disagree, prefer evidence/ if it carries a traceable source. Flag the conflict in any response.
When in-session context contradicts a higher-rank source, trust the higher-rank source and note the discrepancy to Xavier.

conflict_resolution.tension_pattern: Tension: [source-1] says X; [source-2] says Y. Unresolved as of YYYY-MM-DD. Resolution path: [next step].

always: Annotate cited evidence with its tier: [A], [B], or [C].
State 'not in governed docs / not verified' and offer a next step when a business fact cannot be traced to Tier A or B.
Surface conflicts rather than silently resolving them.
never: Treat MCP search results (search, recent_thoughts) as approved business facts.
Promote Tier C directly to a public commitment without Xavier's explicit review.
Override governed-state with a lower-rank source.
